ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning Sensation.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Sensation is that faculty of the soul whereby material objects can be apprehended or discriminated.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the sensoria are the organs or members through which sensations are conveyed.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the objects of sense are the things that come within the province of sensation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And lastly, the subject of sense is the living animal which possesses the faculty of sensation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now there are five senses, and likewise five organs of sense.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The first sense is sight: and the sensoria or organs of sight are the nerves of the brain and the eyes.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now sight is primarily perception of colour, but along with the colour it discriminates the body that has colour, and its size and form, and locality, and the intervening space and the number: also whether it is in motion or at rest, rough or smooth, even or uneven, sharp or blunt, and finally whether its composition is watery or earthy, that is, wet or dry.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The second sense is hearing, whereby voices and sounds are perceived.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And it distinguishes these as sharp or deep, or smooth or loud.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Its organs are the soft nerves of the brain, and the structure of the ears.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, man and the ape are the only animals that do not move their ears.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The third sense is smell, which is caused by the nostrils transmitting the vapours to the brain: and it is bounded by the extreme limits of the anterior ventricle of the brain.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is the faculty by which vapours are perceived and apprehended.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now, the most generic distinction between vapours is whether they have a good or an evil odour, or form an intermediate class with neither a good nor an evil odour.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: A good odour is produced by the thorough digestion in the body of the humours.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: When they are only moderately digested the intermediate class is formed, and when the digestion is very imperfect or utterly wanting, an evil odour results.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The fourth sense is taste: it is the faculty whereby the humours are apprehended or perceived, and its organs of sense are the tongue, and more especially the lips, and the palate (which the Greeks call οὐρανίσκος ), and in these are nerves that come from the brain and are spread out, and convey to the dominant part of the soul the perception or sensation they have encountered.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The so-called gustatory qualities of the humours are these:—sweetness, pungency, bitterness, astringency, acerbity, sourness, saltness, fattiness, stickiness; for taste is capable of discriminating all these.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But water has none of these qualities, and is therefore devoid of taste.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Moreover, astringency is only a more intense and exaggerated form of acerbity.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The fifth sense is touch, which is common to all living things.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Its organs are nerves which come from the brain and ramify all through the body.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Hence the body as a whole, including even the other organs of sense, possesses the sense of touch.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Within its scope come heat and cold, softness and hardness, viscosity and brittleness, heaviness and lightness: for it is by touch alone that these qualities are discriminated.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: On the other hand, roughness and smoothness, dryness and wetness, thickness and thinness, up and down, place and size, whenever that is such as to be embraced in a single application of the sense of touch, are all common to touch and sight, as well as denseness and rareness, that is porosity, and rotundity if it is small, and some other shapes.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In like manner also by the aid of memory and thought perception of the nearness of a body is possible, and similarly perception of number up to two or three, and such small and easily reckoned figures.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But it is by sight rather than touch that these things are perceived.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Creator, it is to be noted, fashioned all the other organs of sense in pairs, so that if one were destroyed, the other might fill its place.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For there are two eyes, two ears, two orifices of the nose, and two tongues, which in some animals, such as snakes, are separate, but in others, like man, are united.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But touch is spread over the whole body with the exception of bones, nerves, nails, horns, hairs, ligaments, and other such structures.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, it is to be observed that sight is possible only in straight lines, whereas smell and hearing are not limited to straight lines only, but act in all directions.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Touch, again, and taste act neither in straight lines, nor in every direction, but only when each comes near to the sensible objects that are proper to it.
